examples.en: It depends on the weather.

an.gotcha: Only used in fixed phrases like 'komme an på' (to depend on).

examples.da: Det er tredje gang, jeg ringer.
examples.en: It is the third time I am calling.
She lives on the third floor.
Harry lives in Copenhagen.

examples.da: Vinden var løj i går aftes.
examples.en: The wind was light last night.

løj.gotcha: Looks like 'løj', the past tense of 'lyve' (lied) — a different word.

examples.en: That is a good sign.
Wait for my signal.

tegn.gotcha: The g is not pronounced as expected; sounds like 'tajn'. Also imperative of 'tegne' (draw).

examples.en: The shirt is completely clean.
It was pure luck.
The fish was still alive.

examples.da: Hun er en meget levende fortæller.
examples.en: She is a very lively storyteller.

examples.da: Skomageren satte skoen på en læst.
examples.en: The shoemaker put the shoe on a last.

læst.gotcha: Identical to 'læst', past participle of 'læse' (read) — context decides.

examples.da: Vi går en tur, medmindre det regner.
examples.en: We will take a walk unless it rains.
We will continue the meeting after lunch.
He continued talking.
The hotel is near the station.
We are very close friends.
Who is the owner of the car?

examples.da: Jul er et gammelt dansk navn.
examples.en: Jul is an old Danish name.

examples.da: Vi fejrer jul hos min mor.
examples.en: We celebrate Christmas at my mother's.
Merry Christmas!

examples.da: Kan du skaffe billetter til koncerten?
examples.en: Can you get tickets for the concert?

examples.da: Mon det bliver regnvejr i morgen?
examples.en: I wonder if it will rain tomorrow?

examples.da: Han har stor respekt for sin lærer.
examples.en: He has great respect for his teacher.
Will you dance with me?
They danced all night.
The children are playing in the garden.

lege.gotcha: 'Lege' is children's play; use 'spille' for games, sports and instruments.

examples.da: Du skal have et kort for at få adgang.
examples.en: You need a card to get access.
No entry.
We eat lunch at twelve o'clock.

frokost.gotcha: Means the midday meal — not breakfast, unlike Norwegian 'frokost' or German 'Frühstück'.

examples.da: Han er forelsket i sin nabo.
examples.en: He is in love with his neighbor.

forelsket.gotcha: Takes the preposition 'i': forelsket i nogen (in love with someone).

examples.da: Ingen kan lide at være en taber.
examples.en: Nobody likes to be a loser.

taber.gotcha: Also the present tense of 'tabe' (loses, drops).

examples.en: It doesn't matter a damn.

skid.gotcha: Vulgar. Do not confuse with Danish 'fart', which innocently means speed.

examples.en: Sit down on a chair.

stol.gotcha: Also imperative of 'stole på' (trust): 'Stol på mig!' means 'Trust me!'

examples.en: The bed is too hard.

examples.da: Det har været en hård uge.
examples.en: It has been a tough week.

hård.gotcha: The d is silent; sounds roughly like 'hor'.

examples.da: Han følte skam over sin fejl.
examples.en: He felt shame over his mistake.
It is quite true, you know.

skam.gotcha: As an adverb it is reassuring ('really, indeed'), not about shame.

examples.da: Jim kommer til festen i aften.
examples.en: Jim is coming to the party tonight.

examples.da: Hun plantede roser i et bed.
examples.en: She planted roses in a flower bed.

bed.gotcha: Identical to 'bed', past tense of 'bide' (bit) and imperative of 'bede'.

examples.da: Min søster er gravid i femte måned.
examples.en: My sister is five months pregnant.

examples.da: Jeg er altid nervøs før en eksamen.
examples.en: I am always nervous before an exam.
In the story, Satan tempts mankind.

examples.da: For satan, hvor gør det ondt!
examples.en: Damn it, that hurts!

satan.gotcha: Strong swear word in Danish; harsher than English 'damn'.

examples.en: Can you close the door?
The shop closes at eight o'clock.

lukke.gotcha: 'Lukke op' means to open — the opposite of what it looks like.

examples.da: Du må vælge mellem de to farver.
examples.en: You have to choose between the two colors.
She chose the red dress.
The waiter brought the bill.

tjener.gotcha: Also the present tense of 'tjene' (earns, serves).

examples.en: The king had great power.
The car was driving at high speed.

examples.da: Der er fart over hende i dag.
examples.en: She is full of energy today.

fart.gotcha: False friend: it means speed, not the English word 'fart'.

examples.da: I nordisk mytologi hersker Hel over dødsriget.
examples.en: In Norse mythology, Hel rules the realm of the dead.
She ate a whole pizza.
We waited a whole hour.
The neighbor is always very friendly.
Kind regards.

examples.da: Du kan enten gå eller cykle.
examples.en: You can either walk or bike.

enten.gotcha: Always paired with 'eller': enten ... eller (either ... or).

examples.da: Der er et hul i min sok.
examples.en: There is a hole in my sock.

hul.gotcha: As an adjective it means hollow.

examples.da: Vi flyver til Paris i morgen.
examples.en: We are flying to Paris tomorrow.
The bird flew away.
The book cost a thousand kroner.
Thanks a lot for the help!
We can walk there together.
O God, hear our prayer.

o.gotcha: Archaic and poetic; not used in everyday Danish.

examples.en: I have bought new shoes.

examples.da: Tag dine sko af i entréen.
examples.en: Take your shoes off in the hallway.

sko.gotcha: Irregular plural: the plural is also 'sko' (en sko, to sko).

examples.da: Hun skal på date i aften.
examples.en: She is going on a date tonight.

examples.da: Jeg er helt enig med dig.
examples.en: I completely agree with you.

examples.da: Vi blev enige om en pris.
examples.en: We agreed on a price.

examples.da: Vi spiser først og går en tur bagefter.
examples.en: We eat first and take a walk afterwards.
The police caught the murderer.
Miss Hansen was our teacher.

frøken.gotcha: Old-fashioned; hardly used in modern Danish outside jokes or old titles.

examples.da: Jeg har en vigtig opgave på arbejdet.
examples.en: I have an important task at work.
The pupils solve exercises in math.
He is seriously ill.
Are you serious about that?

examples.da: Han opførte sig som en nar.
examples.en: He behaved like a fool.

nar.gotcha: Do not confuse with 'når' (when) — the å changes the word.

examples.en: Hey, you forgot your bag!

examples.da: Hesten har mistet en sko fra sin hov.
examples.en: The horse has lost a shoe from its hoof.

examples.da: Richard arbejder på et kontor i Aarhus.
examples.en: Richard works in an office in Aarhus.

examples.da: Hun lagde hele sin sjæl i arbejdet.
examples.en: She put her whole soul into the work.

examples.da: Det er en helt særlig dag.
examples.en: It is a very special day.

examples.da: Kan jeg få en ekstra pude?
examples.en: Can I have an extra pillow?

examples.da: Vi mødtes på en bar i byen.
examples.en: We met at a bar in town.
He walked with bare feet.
Go away, and never come back!
The book is lying on the table.
The house is situated by the sea.

ligge.gotcha: Easily confused with 'lægge' (to lay something down); 'ligge' takes no object.

examples.da: Du skal ikke bekymre dig om mig.
examples.en: You should not worry about me.

bekymre.gotcha: Usually reflexive: 'bekymre sig om' (to worry about).

examples.en: The wall is quite low.
Prices are low in the winter.

lav.gotcha: Also the imperative of 'lave' (make): 'Lav mad!' means 'Make food!'

examples.da: Du må ikke røre ved billederne.
examples.en: You must not touch the pictures.
Stir the dough with a spoon.

examples.da: Vi fik besøg af mine forældre.
examples.en: We had a visit from my parents.
Thanks for the visit!

examples.da: Han vandt, og det var endda hans første løb.
examples.en: He won, and it was even his first race.
Apparently he has gone home.
Tommy plays football every Saturday.

examples.da: Jesus er en central figur i kristendommen.
examples.en: Jesus is a central figure in Christianity.

examples.da: Egernet gemte en nød i træet.
examples.en: The squirrel hid a nut in the tree.

nød.gotcha: Identical to 'nød', past tense of 'nyde' (enjoyed), and 'nød' meaning distress.

examples.en: Look, there he is!

examples.da: Jeg kan lide frugt, især jordbær.
examples.en: I like fruit, especially strawberries.

examples.da: Ifølge vejrudsigten bliver det sol i morgen.
examples.en: According to the forecast, it will be sunny tomorrow.
There were at least a hundred people.
Denmark has had many kings.

examples.da: Jeg har brug for otte timers søvn.
examples.en: I need eight hours of sleep.

examples.da: Børnene vil gerne have en is.
examples.en: The children would like an ice cream.

examples.da: Der er is på søen om vinteren.
examples.en: There is ice on the lake in winter.
She is a smart girl.
It was a wise decision.
The newspaper has many loyal readers.

examples.da: Giv lige bilen et tjek før turen.
examples.en: Give the car a check before the trip.
The house caught fire.

examples.da: Har du ild? Jeg mangler en lighter.
examples.en: Do you have a light? I need a lighter.

examples.da: Vær nu ikke sådan et fjols!
examples.en: Don't be such a fool!

fjols.gotcha: Neuter gender: 'et fjols', even though it refers to a person.

examples.en: The soldier followed his order.

examples.da: Firmaet fik en stor ordre i dag.
examples.en: The company received a big order today.
We remember our old friends.

vore.gotcha: Formal or old-fashioned; everyday Danish uses 'vores' instead.

examples.da: Kagen har form som et hjerte.
examples.en: The cake is shaped like a heart.

examples.da: Jeg er i god form for tiden.
examples.en: I am in good shape these days.
He lost control of the car.
There was a ticket inspection on the train.
The apartment has three rooms.

examples.da: Raketten blev sendt ud i rummet.
examples.en: The rocket was sent into space.

rum.gotcha: Unchanged plural: et rum, flere rum.

examples.en: The grill needs a lighter.

tænder.gotcha: Identical to 'tænder' meaning teeth (plural of 'tand') and 'lights' (present of 'tænde').

examples.da: Hun er lykkelig i sit nye job.
examples.en: She is happy in her new job.

examples.da: De levede lykkeligt til deres dages ende.
examples.en: They lived happily ever after.

examples.da: Er der en bank i nærheden?
examples.en: Is there a bank nearby?

nærheden.gotcha: Almost only used in the fixed phrase 'i nærheden' (nearby).

examples.da: Han spiller fodbold med sine kammerater.
examples.en: He plays football with his mates.
Darn! I forgot my keys.

examples.da: Vil du have et glas vand?
examples.en: Would you like a glass of water?
The bottle is made of glass.

examples.da: Kunstneren vil skabe noget helt nyt.
examples.en: The artist wants to create something completely new.

skabe.gotcha: Also the plural of 'skab' (cupboards). 'Skabe sig' means to make a fuss.

examples.da: Pas på, du ikke falder på isen.
examples.en: Be careful you don't fall on the ice.
The leaves fell from the trees.
I am not particularly hungry.

examples.da: Hun er populær blandt sine kolleger.
examples.en: She is popular among her colleagues.

examples.da: Vi så blandt andet Den Lille Havfrue.
examples.en: Among other things, we saw the Little Mermaid.
That was damn well done!

fandeme.gotcha: Vulgar intensifier; avoid in polite or formal situations.

examples.en: Mark comes from England.
The cows are grazing in the field.
The farmer plows his field.

examples.da: Vi skal til Paris i efterårsferien.
examples.en: We are going to Paris during the autumn holiday.

examples.da: Kan du bevise, at du har ret?
examples.en: Can you prove that you are right?

examples.da: I så tilfælde bliver vi hjemme.
examples.en: In that case, we will stay home.

examples.da: Det var et rent tilfælde, at vi mødtes.
examples.en: It was pure coincidence that we met.
The soup is very hot.

hed.gotcha: Identical to 'hed', past tense of 'hedde' (was called) — very common in texts.

examples.da: Hun tjener gode penge i sit job.
examples.en: She earns good money in her job.

examples.da: Han tjente i hæren i to år.
examples.en: He served in the army for two years.

examples.da: Du må ikke lyve for mig.
examples.en: You must not lie to me.
He lied about his age.

lyve.gotcha: Irregular past tense: løj, løjet. Only means telling untruths, never lying down.

examples.da: Jeg drikker hverken kaffe eller te.
examples.en: I drink neither coffee nor tea.

hverken.gotcha: Always paired with 'eller': hverken ... eller (neither ... nor).

examples.en: She has a strong will.

examples.da: Han fik sin vilje til sidst.
examples.en: He got his way in the end.
We often see each other at the swimming pool.

examples.da: Jeg vil gerne takke jer for hjælpen.
examples.en: I would like to thank you for the help.

examples.da: Vi tog toget til Odense og cyklede derfra.
examples.en: We took the train to Odense and biked from there.

examples.da: Kan du bringe pakken til posthuset?
examples.en: Can you take the package to the post office?

bringe.gotcha: Irregular past tense: bragte, bragt. Everyday Danish often prefers 'tage med' instead.

examples.da: Husk at tage din medicin hver morgen.
examples.en: Remember to take your medicine every morning.
The cake tastes really delicious.
He is a hot guy.

examples.da: Den stakkels hund stod ude i regnen.
examples.en: The poor dog was standing out in the rain.

stakkels.gotcha: Never inflects despite the -s ending: den stakkels mand, de stakkels børn.

examples.en: The brain uses a lot of energy.

hjerne.gotcha: Do not confuse with 'hjerte' (heart) — they look similar.

examples.da: Jeg drømte om dig i nat.
examples.en: I dreamt about you last night.

examples.da: Hun drømmer om at rejse verden rundt.
examples.en: She dreams of traveling around the world.

examples.da: Man må vælge det mindste af to onder.
examples.en: One must choose the lesser of two evils.
Take a deep breath.
He slept deeply all night.

examples.da: Prøv at undgå de travle timer.
examples.en: Try to avoid the busy hours.
The accident could not be avoided.
What a lovely day!
The food was really delicious.

examples.da: Er du parat til at tage af sted?
examples.en: Are you ready to leave?
